channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Replaces Expiring Tariffs With Forced Labour Duties
item.description: The Trump administration is imposing new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on 60 trading partners, including the European Union, over allegations of lax enforcement of forced labour bans. This move replaces ex...

item.title: ICC Dismisses War Crimes Case Against Sudan Rebel Due to Evidence Concerns
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has decided to drop the war crimes case against Sudanese rebel Abdallah Banda, who was accused of attacking African Union peacekeepers in Darfur nearly two decad...

item.title: Comelec warns candidates against premature campaigning in Dasmariñas special polls
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has issued a warning to candidates in the special elections of Dasmariñas, Cavite, prohibiting premature campaigning before the official campaign period starts on...

item.title: Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony: Royal Arrival and Team Australia's Grand Entrance
item.description: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a unique and entertaining twist as King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in a Dr Who Tardis, delighting the crowd. This creative choice...

item.title: Centre appoints new secretaries in education and other ministries as part of a major bureaucratic reshuffle
item.description: The Union government carried out a significant bureaucratic reshuffle on Thursday by announcing 13 secretary-level appointments across various ministries and departments. Among the most closely watche...

item.title: Nigerian Army Declares Private Wanted Over Alleged Illegal Supply of Military Uniforms to Criminal Groups
item.description: The Nigerian Army has declared one of its personnel, Private Mohammed Yusuf Amutu, wanted over allegations that he unlawfully sold and supplied military uniforms to terrorists and other criminal eleme...

item.title: House Oversight Committee Demands Investigation into Trump Family's Ties with MAGA Brothers Facing Serious Charges
item.description: Rep. Yassamin Ansari (D-AZ) has demanded the House Oversight Committee to investigate the connections between the Trump family and the Tate brothers, who face severe charges including rape, human traf...

item.title: Trump Comments on Discombobulation in Oval Office During World Series Event
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump made a controversial remark during a White House reception for the Los Angeles Dodgers, who won the Major League Baseball (MLB) World Series. Trump described the Oval Offic...

item.title: Canadian mathematician Jacob Tsimerman awarded Fields Medal for groundbreaking work in arithmetic geometry
item.description: Jacob Tsimerman, a mathematics professor at the University of Toronto, has been awarded the Fields Medal, the most prestigious honor in mathematics, for his contributions to arithmetic and complex alg...

item.title: Trump Threatens Retaliation Amid Houthi Attacks on Saudi Oil Tankers and Rising Global Oil Prices
item.description: US President Donald Trump has warned Iran of 'major military punishment' following attacks by the Houthi militia on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. The attacks, which occurred near the Saudi port of...

item.title: AC Milan Extend Luka Modric's Contract by One Season
item.description: Croatian veteran Luka Modric has officially extended his contract with AC Milan by one season, keeping him at the Serie A club until June 30, 2027. The midfielder, who turns 41 in less than two months...

item.title: Former Bosasa Whistleblower Seeks to Testify on Political Interference Allegations
item.description: Angelo Agrizzi, a former Bosasa chief operating officer and whistleblower, has formally requested permission to testify before the Madlanga Commission under witness protection. He alleges political in...

item.title: Artists from Across Africa Convene at First-Ever Global Summit in Ethiopia
item.description: The article highlights the gathering of Africa's top artists at the inaugural World Public Summit in Ethiopia, emphasizing cultural collaboration and innovation. The event brings together creatives fr...

item.title: Trump Blames Senate Leader Thune for Legislative Stalemates
item.description: President Donald Trump is escalating his frustration with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD), according to a Republican operative. Trump's anger stems from the Senate's inability to pass his SAV...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Expect Impeachment Trial to Be Based on Law and Evidence
item.description: A July 2026 survey by OCTA Research revealed that 71% of Filipinos prefer senators to base their decision on law and evidence rather than political considerations during the impeachment trial of Vice ...

item.title: Winnipeg man arrested for waving replica BB handgun through sunroof on Trans-Canada Highway
item.description: A 25-year-old Winnipeg man was arrested by Manitoba RCMP after waving a replica BB handgun through his vehicle's sunroof while driving on the Trans-Canada Highway. Police received a report of an errat...

item.title: Furbo 360: A Comprehensive Guide to Dog Camera Features and Benefits
item.description: The Furbo 360 is a smart pet camera designed to enhance the experience of owning a dog. It combines advanced technology with interactive features, allowing owners to monitor their pets remotely while ...

item.title: The Drawbacks of Banning Social Media for Children
item.description: The article argues against the blanket ban on social media for children, highlighting how such policies ignore the platform's role in education, communication, and mental health support. While concern...

item.title: Tanzania Boosts Tourism and Industrial Growth
item.description: The article discusses Tanzanian officials' efforts to enhance tourism relations with South Korea through diplomatic channels. Kijaji, a key government representative, emphasized the need for the embas...

item.title: Sigabo Episode 24: Gabo and Sam Successfully Complete Mission to Capture Mr. Chan
item.description: The article discusses the successful conclusion of a mission in the TV series 'Sigabo' (Episode 24), where characters Gabo and Sam achieve their goal of capturing Mr. Chan. Published on July 23, 2026,...
